steps.title: Place though between clauses
steps.content: The sentence first says that jetties can sometimes have the opposite effect. It then gives a full clause explaining how obstructing sand flow can increase erosion. The word though adds contrast and must be set off before the second independent clause.

steps.title: Check the clause boundary
steps.content: Choice A leaves the second independent clause attached only by commas. Choice C lacks enough punctuation before the second clause. Choice D puts the semicolon before though, disrupting the contrast phrase. The answer is B.
